Addresses and more
Information entered into online forms, such as phone numbers, email addresses and some physical addresses.
JSON
Bookmarks
The pages bookmarked in Chrome.
HTML
Bookmarks are viewable in any web browser and can be imported by the majority of web browsers, including Firefox and Internet Explorer. Bookmarks are exported as HTML files in a standardised format used by Netscape and supported by several other browsers when importing bookmarks (Firefox, Internet Explorer, etc.).
History
The sites visited in Chrome, including web addresses entered in the address bar.
JSON
Dictionary
Words added to Chrome dictionary.
CSV
The exported CSV has a single column with one dictionary word per row. Note that if the data is encrypted, the word Encrypted is written instead.
Extensions
Extensions installed from the Chrome Web Store.
JSON
Settings
Chrome user settings.
JSON
Reading list
Pages added to the reading list in Chrome.
HTML
Reading list pages are exported as HTML files in a standardised format used by Netscape and supported by several other browsers when importing bookmarks (Firefox, Internet Explorer, etc.).
OS settings
ChromeOS settings. Not exported in third-party takeout.
JSON
Device Information
Information about user devices that are running a sync-enabled Chrome Browser. Not exported in third-party takeout.
JSON

Starred in Android.vcf

Data included

Contacts and contact photos that you added yourself, as well as contacts saved from your interactions in Google products like Gmail.

Folder structure

Now that you’ve downloaded the contacts from your Google Account, you’ll find folders named 'My Contacts' and 'All Contacts', plus additional folders if you created any labels or starred contacts on your Android device. The 'My Contacts' folder contains only those contacts that you added yourself. The 'All Contacts' folder contains both contacts that you added yourself as well as contact info automatically saved from your interactions in Google products, like Gmail.

How to handle this export

If you downloaded your contacts as a CSV file, you can view them in a spreadsheet, or import them to Microsoft Outlook or another Google Account. If you downloaded your contacts as a vCard file, you can import them to another Google Account, Mac OS, iOS or other contacts services.

You're in control of your data in Contacts

Review your data

You can review contacts that you’ve added to your Google Account in Contacts

Manage your data

You can delete contacts from your Google Account in Contacts

Control your data

Go to Gmail settings to control whether contacts are created for auto-complete.Go to your Google Account to control whether contact info is saved when you interact with people in other Google products.

More options to manage your privacy

Google Account

Manage your info, privacy and make Google work better for you
Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
Contacts
CSV
For importing to MS Outlook or another Google Account
vCard
A common file format for electronic business cards used by programs such as Gmail, Microsoft Outlook and Mac OS X contacts.

Fit

0 files exported successfully

No data found

Data included

Your Google Fit data, including your workouts, sleep data, and daily metrics like steps and distance.

Folder structure

The export could contain up to four folders:
  • Activities: Workouts and automatically tracked activities, like running or cycling.
  • Daily activity metrics: Daily totals for each activity metric, like steps and distance.
  • All data: All your data from Google Fit, grouped by source.
  • All sessions: All the sessions recorded in Journal, including activities and sleep.

More options to manage your privacy

Google Account

Manage your info, privacy and make Google work better for you
Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
Activities
Workouts and automatically tracked activities, like running or cycling.
TCX
A common file format for fitness data, which you can use with other fitness apps like Garmin Connect and Strava.
Daily activity metrics
Daily totals for each activity metric, like steps and distance.
CSV
Fields in your daily aggregation files will include: start time, end time, calories (kcal), distance (m), average heart rate (bpm), max heart rate (bpm), min heart rate (bpm), low latitude (deg), low longitude (deg), high latitude (deg), high longitude (deg), average speed (m/s), max speed (m/s), min speed (m/s), step count, average weight (kg), max weight (kg), min weight (kg), Move Minutes count, Heart Points, inactive duration (ms), walking duration (ms)
All data
All your data from Google Fit, grouped by source.
JSON
All sessions
All the sessions recorded in Journal, including activities and sleep.
JSON

Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
Sending and requesting money
Your send and request transaction history
CSV
Fields in your Money Send and Requests file will include: Time, Transaction ID, Description, Memo, Payment method, Status and Amount.
My activity
Your Google Pay activity visible at myactivity.google.com. This includes online and in-shop activity made with virtual account numbers.
HTML
Saved passes
Your saved passes such as boarding passes, event tickets and loyalty cards
PDF
Google transactions
Your purchases on Google, such as Play and YouTube, and purchases made using Google Pay balance.
CSV
Fields in your Transactions file will include: time, transaction ID, description, product, payment method, status and amount.
Chat messages on Google Pay
Your chat messages sent to other Google Pay users, merchants and so on.
JSON
Your chat messages file will be organised as a list of conversations. The structure of each conversation may differ slightly based on the conversation partner.
Your activity from Spots on Google Pay
Description and status of your activity, such as purchases, as reported by the Spot provider.
JSON
A JSON list of your purchases made in Spots with description and status, as reported by the Spot provider.
Rewards earned
Collection of rewards earned by you such as cashback rewards using Google Pay.
CSV
A list of CSV files representing your rewards earned on Google Pay. Each CSV captures a particular type of reward.
Group expenses
Collection of group expenses of which you are a participant.
JSON/JPEG/PNG
Your group expenses will be formatted to a JSON file with all the details of the expenses and expense items. All receipt attachments will be organised in a receipt folder.
Social campaign data
Collection of social campaign data that you generated during the campaigns.
JSON
Your social campaign data will be grouped by campaigns. The detailed structure depends on each campaign and may contain different items for various campaigns.

print-subscriptions.json
shared_album_comments.json
user-generated-memory-titles.json

Data included

Your photos and videos from Google Photos.

Folder structure

You will find folders containing your photos and videos in Google Photos, including related metadata. Please go to takeout.google.com if you are looking for photos from other Google services.

You're in control of your data in Google Photos

Review your data

Review your content from Google Photos here.

Manage your data

Delete your content from Google Photos here.

Control your data

You can control the automatic upload of content to Google Photos using the Back up & sync setting on your device. For more information, see here.You can control the collection of data to other Google services in that service.

More options to manage your privacy

Google Account

Manage your info, privacy and make Google work better for you
Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
Photos
Unedited and edited photos contained in each album.
Original format/PNG/JPG/WEBP
Photos uploaded in original quality will be provided in their original format. Photos uploaded in high quality may be returned in a different format.

Photos that have been edited are downloaded with the original file name ending with '-edited'.
Videos
Videos contained in each album.
Original format/MP4
Videos uploaded in original quality will be provided in their original format. Videos uploaded in high quality may be returned in a different format.
Album metadata
Data associated with each album, such as title or description.
JSON
Json is an open-standard file format that provides data in human readable form.
Photo metadata
Data associated with each photo or video, such as creation time or comments.
JSON
Json is an open-standard file format that provides data in human readable form.

Google Play Games Services

22 files exported successfully
Some file formats (e.g. html, png) will be viewable in your browser, and will appear as clickable links below that will open the file once you've fully extracted your archive. Other file types can be opened by their appropriate applications by navigating through the archive's directory. You can click on any folders that may be present to see their contents.

Data that is still in the process of being deleted is not included in your archive. Learn more about your deleted data.
Activity.html
Recall.html

Activity.html

Recall.html
Avatar.png
Player.html

Data included

Data, including achievements and scores, from games that you play

Folder structure

The export contains two main folders:
  • Global: information about the player
  • Games: a directory containing folders for each game for which the user has data

More options to manage your privacy

Google Account

Manage your info, privacy and make Google work better for you
Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
avatar.png
The avatar picture used by the player.
PNG
Player Information
The player profile settings, as set in the Play Games App Settings screen.
HTML
JSON
Scores
Details of the high scores that we have stored for the player/game in question.
HTML
JSON
Achievements
Details of the achievements that we have stored for the player/game in question.
HTML
JSON
Quests
Details of the quests that we have stored for the player/game in question.
HTML
JSON
Experience
Details of the experience point earning events that we have stored for the player/game in question.
HTML
JSON
Activity
Details of the activity (e.g. first/last time played) that we have stored for the player/game in question.
HTML
JSON
CoverImage
An image (usually a screenshot) generated by the game when the game was saved.
PNG/JPG
Data.bin
The actual saved game data
Original format
Metadata
Metadata associated with the saved game (date/time, description etc.).
HTML
JSON
name.bin
The actual saved game data.
Original format

Installs

JSON

Google Podcasts

0 files exported successfully

No data found

Data included

Podcasts that you are subscribed to.

Folder structure

Each type of Podcasts data is in its own subfolder.

How to handle this export

The export contains an OPML file with subscriptions. It can be used to import subscriptions to another Podcasts application or service.

You're in control of your data in Google Podcasts

Review your data

Review your Google Podcasts activity data

Manage your data

Manage your subscriptions

Control your data

Manage your Google Podcasts settings

Other file types can be opened by their appropriate applications by navigating through the archive's directory. You can click on any folders that may be present to see their contents.

Addresses.txt
Loyalty.txt
Merchant Reviews.txt
Order Preferred Contact Emails.txt
Orders.txt
Product Reviews.txt
Save for Later Cart from Youtube.txt
Shopping Cart from Google Shopping.txt
Shopping Cart from Youtube.txt

Data included

Google Shopping order history, loyalty, addresses and reviews.

More options to manage your privacy

Google Account

Manage your info, privacy and make Google work better for you
Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
Order history data
Detail of your orders in Google Shopping.
JSON
A JSON-formatted array of Google Shopping order history, for example: 
{
  "orderId": "G-SHP-1234-56-7890",
  "orderDatetime": "2019-02-05T15:25:35.500Z",
  "merchantName": "Merchant Name",
  "items": [{
    "description": "Item One",
    "quantity": 1,
    "price": "$29.99",
    "discount": "$0.00",
    "tax": "$2.99",
    "state": "DELIVERED",
  }, {
    "description": "Item Two",
    "quantity": 1,
    "price": "$19.99",
    "discount": "$0.00",
    "tax": "$1.99",
    "state": "DELIVERED",
  }],
  "orderStatus": "ALL_DELIVERED",
  "orderTotal": "$54.96",
  "orderDiscount": "$0.00",
  "orderFees": "$0.00",
  "orderAdjustment": "$0.00",
  "orderRefund": "",
  "orderRecipient": "",
  "deliveryAddress": "1600 Amphitheatre Parkway,  Mountain View, CA 94043, United States",
  "billingInstrument": "VISA-1111",
}
Loyalty data
Your loyalty data in Google Shopping.
CSV
A text file listing the merchant name, loyalty code and loyalty user.
Addresses data
Your address data in Google Shopping.
CSV
A text file listing the recipient name and address.
Product reviews data
Your product reviews data.
CSV
A text file listing the rating, comment and creation timestamp.
Merchant reviews data
Your merchant reviews data.
CSV
A text file listing the rating, comment and creation timestamp.
Shopping basket data
Shopping basket of the user.
CSV
A text file listing the items in your shopping basket.

Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
Application listings
Application listings published on Google Workspace Marketplace: https://workspace.google.com/marketplace
JSON
The application listing is exported to developers in JSON format. One file is created per app listing.
Reviews
Reviews about apps published on Google Workspace Marketplace: https://workspace.google.com/marketplace
JSON
Reviews provided by the logged-in user are exported in a file in JSON format.

Automations.json
GoogleNestConnect.json
GoogleNestPartnerConnections.json
HomeApp.json
HomeHistory.json
SecurityAlarmClips.json
SoundSensing.json

Data included

Device, room, home and history information from the Home app.

Folder structure

Files containing all of the Home app data. If there are audio recordings from sound-sensing enabled devices, they will be placed in a 'blobs' folder.

You're in control of your data in Home App

Review your data

Review your Home App data

Manage your data

Delete your Home App data.

More options to manage your privacy

Google Account

Manage your info, privacy and make Google work better for you
Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
Home App data
Devices, rooms and homes.
JSON
'Devices' and 'Rooms' are contained in 'RoomsAndDevices' objects, which are contained in 'FullStructure' objects. Devices not in a structure are included as 'unassigned_devices'.
Home history data
History of events in your home.
JSON
Events in your home are listed under 'structure_history'. Events about a specific device are listed under 'device_history'. 'Timestamp' is the time of the event.
Audio recordings
Audio recordings at the time that sound-sensing devices detected activity.
audio/aac
Audio recordings are found in the 'blobs' folder and end with the '.aac' suffix.
Security alarm clip data
List of security alarm clip data that belongs to your home.
JSON
All the clip data is under an array named 'securityAlarmClipData' which is contained in another array named 'securityAlarmData‘
Professional services data
Information about professional services (e.g. installation services) that you have ordered.
JSON
'partnerName' is the name of the partner offering the professional service. 'installation.location' is the location where the installation service is taking place. 'installation.deviceSettings' are the default settings that you have selected for the devices set up during the installation.
Automations data
Information about Automations in homes.
JSON
'script' is the name of the script containing automations created by script editor

Google Account

Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
Records
Your Timeline data if you haven't started using the new version of Timeline.
JSON
Your location data when Timeline is turned on.
KML
Your location data when Timeline is turned on, in the KML format. You can load your KML file into Google Earth to view locations that you have visited.
Semantic Location History
Your Semantic Location History data consisting of inferred place visits and activity segments from Timeline, if you haven't started using the new version of Timeline.
JSON
Settings
Your settings.
JSON
Tombstones
Your deletion markers.
CSV
Your Tombstones are temporarily stored to delete Timeline time ranges across Google systems and your devices.
Timeline edits and related data
Your edits and related data, including the original place visits and activity segments and associated raw signals used to infer them, and the updated place visits and activity segments.
JSON
Backups
A file indicating whether you have encrypted Timeline backups stored on Google servers.
TXT

Mail

1 file exported successfully
Some file formats (e.g. html, png) will be viewable in your browser, and will appear as clickable links below that will open the file once you've fully extracted your archive. Other file types can be opened by their appropriate applications by navigating through the archive's directory. You can click on any folders that may be present to see their contents.

Data that is still in the process of being deleted is not included in your archive. Learn more about your deleted data.

Data included

Messages and attachments in your Gmail account in MBOX format. User settings from your Gmail account in JSON format.

Folder structure

For user settings, we provide a separate folder with each exported setting in its individual JSON file. For messages, we provide all of your mail in a single MBOX file by default. Alternatively, you can choose labels to export, each of which will be contained in its own MBOX file. When you export your email from Gmail, the labels of each message are preserved in a special X-Gmail-Labels header, in CSV format. While no mail client recognizes this header now, most mail clients allow for extensions to be written that could make use of this data.

How to handle this export

For more information about importing mboxes into your favourite mail client, here are some non-Google tutorials for Thunderbird and Apple Mail.

You're in control of your data in Mail

Review your data

You can review your mailbox in the Gmail UI.

Manage your data

You can delete your entire Gmail account by following this help article. You can delete individual messages in the Gmail UI.

More options to manage your privacy

Google Account

Manage your info, privacy and make Google work better for you
Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
Email messages
MBOX
An mbox is a way of storing mail messages and is supported by common mail clients such as Microsoft Outlook, Mozilla Thunderbird and Apple's Mail program. These files are most easily opened by a dedicated mail client such as those listed above.
User settings
JSON
A standard lightweight data-interchange format.

Photos contribution

Post Trip answers
Your answers to questions following a navigation session.
JSON

Reviews.json

Data included

Records of your starred places and place reviews.

Folder structure

One file for starred places and another file for place reviews.

You're in control of your data in Maps (your places)

Review your data

Review your place reviews

More options to manage your privacy

Google Account

Manage your info, privacy and make Google work better for you
Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
Starred places
Places that were starred as a favourite.
GeoJSON
A file created with GeoJSON format. Google provides a 'FeatureCollection' containing a 'Feature' element for each contribution being downloaded. See https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7946 for documentation of the standard fields in the GeoJSON format, and see the field-level explanations for the Google-defined 'properties' members of the 'Feature' element. Note that the information about the contributed place reflects Google's current understanding of the world, and not when the contribution was made; e.g. the name of a restaurant may have changed between when the review was originally written and now.
Reviews
Reviews (with or without text) left for a place.
GeoJSON
A file created with GeoJSON format. Google provides a 'FeatureCollection' containing a 'Feature' element for each contribution being downloaded. See https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7946 for documentation of the standard fields in the GeoJSON format, and see the field-level explanations for the Google-defined 'properties' members of the 'Feature' element. Note that the information about the contributed place reflects Google's current understanding of the world, and not when the contribution was made; e.g. the name of a restaurant may have changed between when the review was originally written and now.

Favourite images.csv

Data included

Collections of saved links (images, places, web pages etc.) from Google Search and Maps.

Folder structure

One folder with a CSV per collection and an image file per image that you uploaded or that was uploaded to your collection.

How to handle this export

Copy and paste URLs into any browser to view saved items.

You're in control of your data in Saved

Review your data

Review your collections of saved items.

Manage your data

Delete collections of saved items.

More options to manage your privacy

Google Account

Manage your info, privacy and make Google work better for you
Below are all the possible formats that you could have received in your archive:
Collection of saved items
Title, URL, note and comments of the saved item
CSV
CSV contains the following fields: Title, Note, content URL and Comment. If the collection has a description, the first line is the collection description followed by a blank line. If there are comments on the collection, they go in the Comment field after the collection description (and blank line) before the item details. If there is more than one comment per item, the other item fields (Title, Note and URL) are repeated for each item comment.
Uploaded images
Images the user uploaded or were uploaded to their collection
IMAGE
The image is in the format that the user uploaded it. For example, if the user uploaded a JPEG, the output is a JPEG and if they uploaded a PNG, the output is a PNG.

Data included

Images and videos you have uploaded to Google Street View

Task
Your Google Tasks
JSON

The json representing a Task has the following format:

{
  "kind" : "",
  "items" : [ {
    "kind" : "",
    "id" : "",
    "title" : "",
    "updated" : "",
    "selfLink" : "",
    "items" : [ {
      "kind" : "",
      "id" : "",
      "title" : "",
      "updated" : "",
      "selfLink" : "",
      "status" : "",
    } ]
  } ]
}
